what: Tiger side-head embedded into the deployed M2 INT8 graph (branch tiger-module-embed)
additive: released M2.onnx / bundle / v1.0-final untouched; work on a branch only

T1_head_in_M2_space.method: A2 linear head (logistic regression) recomputed in M2 space
T1_head_in_M2_space.eval: ATRW tiger (detection) vs CCT cis_val_clean background, held-out test

T3_parity.input: identical golden preprocessed tensors (tiger / CCT empty / CCT bobcat)
T3_parity.verdict: bit-identical across Python, C++ (gx10), C++ (real Pi)
T3_parity.note: 16-class head is blind to tiger (argmax=empty); the side-head catches it and does not fire on a real CCT cat (bobcat)

T3_pi_latency.host: Raspberry Pi CM5, governor performance, throttled=0x0, threads=1, ORT infer only
T3_pi_latency.conclusion: variants statistically indistinguishable; head cost ~1-2 us per bench_head.c (~0.01% of infer)

next_step: wire cpp/src/session.cpp to read tiger_score + a target-registry/policy entry to emit the shutter
